attributes.title: Asar
attributes.role: god associated with death and resurrection

entities.name: The Ark

attributes.symbolism: symbol of Christ; later symbol of goddess

entities.raw_quote: The Ark a symbol of Christ—Events of the Deluge incorporated with the revived Idolatry—The Ark became the symbol of the goddess

entities.name: Deluge

attributes.symbolism: type of regeneration; basis for baptism

entities.raw_quote: The Deluge the type of regeneration—Baptism of the Lesser Mysteries

entities.name: Greater Mysteries

attributes.purpose: revelation of the god; preparation via fasting and confession

entities.raw_quote: Description of Greater Mysteries—Their object the revelation of the god

entities.name: Apporeta
entities.raw_quote: The dread secret or "Apporeta"—Revelation of the god as the Serpent

entities.name: daimonia

attributes.role: inhabit idols; worshipped as spirits of the dead

entities.raw_quote: The worship of the dead a stepping-stone to worship of daimonia

entities.name: human sacrifices

attributes.method: by fire or by cross; offered for sins

entities.raw_quote: Human sacrifices to the Pagan god by the Cross or by fire, both being symbols or manifestations of the god

entities.name: Round Cake

attributes.symbolism: symbol of Sun god; offered for sins

entities.raw_quote: The Round Cake, the symbol of the Sun god, offered for the sins of the people

entities.name: Purgatory

attributes.function: linked to sacrifices for the dead

entities.raw_quote: Sacrifices for the dead — Peuance — The Mysteries

entities.name: Mysteries
entities.raw_quote: Initiates prepared by fasting and confession

entities.name: Serpent

attributes.role: form of revelation in Apporeta; identification with Sun god

entities.raw_quote: Revelation of the god as the Serpent

entities.name: Sun god
entities.raw_quote: The Sun as God... The dead king... regarded as Son of God and therefore as the Sun—Goddess regarded as the Earth and Moon

summary: This chunk presents a theological-historical argument linking ancient pagan religious systems—including Egyptian, Babylonian, and other Near Eastern traditions—to a corrupted form of primal monotheism. It emphasizes the worship of the dead as a stepping-stone to spirit worship (daimonia), describes ritual practices such as the Greater Mysteries, human sacrifice, and offerings for the dead (including Purgatory), and identifies Osiris/Asar, Semiramis, Zerbanit, and the Sun god (often identified with the Serpent) as central deities. The text frames these traditions as part of a deliberate post-Flood restoration of idolatry that perverts original patriarchal faith.
key_passages: "The worship of the dead a stepping-stone to worship of daimonia—Influence of priesthood through their magical powers"
"Idols the habitation of the daimonia — Testimony of Spiritualists and Buddhists — Haunted houses, etc."
"Human sacrifices to the Pagan god by the Cross or by fire, both being symbols or manifestations of the god"
"The Round Cake, the symbol of the Sun god, offered for the sins of the people Purgatory — Sacrifices for the dead — Peuance"
"Initiates prepared by fasting and confession—Description of Greater Mysteries—Their object the revelation of the god—The dread secret or "Apporeta"—Revelation of the god as the Serpent"
